skill.name: background-removal
skill.description: AI Background Removal - Remove background from images, create transparent PNG. Supports JPG, PNG, WebP local files and remote URLs. One credit per image.
skill.summary: Remove image backgrounds with AI via verging.ai. Returns transparent PNG.
skill.author: verging.ai

security_audit.summary: This skill uses curl to call the verging.ai background removal API. Static analysis flagged 60 potential issues, but the vast majority are false positives triggered by bash code blocks in documentation that demonstrate API usage. The only real risk is that the skill requires a VERGING_API_KEY env var, makes outbound network requests, and writes to /tmp/verging-bg-removal/. No evidence of obfuscation, data exfiltration, prompt injection, or malicious intent. The skill performs a clearly documented, single-purpose operation: uploading an image to verging.ai and returning a transparent PNG. No prompt injection attempts were detected in the skill files.

medium_findings.title: Requires API key and sends user images to external service
medium_findings.description: The skill requires users to set a VERGING_API_KEY environment variable and uploads user-provided images to verging.ai for processing. This is a legitimate single-purpose operation, but users should be aware that their images are sent to a third-party service.

low_findings.title: Uses shell command execution via curl in bash code blocks
low_findings.description: The skill documentation contains numerous curl command examples. These are documentation patterns showing the user how to interact with the verging.ai API, not actual executable code in the skill. The skill relies on the user invoking curl commands as guided by the AI assistant.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded API endpoint URLs
low_findings.description: The skill uses hardcoded URLs pointing to verging.ai API endpoints. This is expected for a skill that integrates with a specific external service. URLs are clearly documented and not obfuscated.

low_findings.title: Temporary directory usage for image processing
low_findings.description: The skill writes downloaded images and results to /tmp/verging-bg-removal/. This is a reasonable pattern for handling temporary image files during processing.

content.user_title: Remove Image Backgrounds Instantly with AI
content.value_statement: Manually removing image backgrounds is slow and requires expensive software. This skill automates background removal using AI, delivering transparent PNGs from any local file or URL in seconds.
actual_capabilities: Removes backgrounds from JPG, JPEG, PNG, and WebP images
Supports both local file paths and remote image URLs as input
Returns results as transparent PNG files
Downloads remote images automatically when given a URL
Polls job status and returns the result URL
limitations: Maximum file size is 10MB per image
Requires a valid verging.ai API key
Images are sent to verging.ai servers for processing

use_cases.title: E-commerce product photos
use_cases.description: Remove backgrounds from product images to create clean catalog photos with transparent backgrounds.

use_cases.title: Social media graphics
use_cases.description: Quickly isolate subjects from photos to create custom graphics for posts, stories, and marketing materials.

use_cases.title: Batch photo editing
use_cases.description: Process multiple images efficiently through the API for projects requiring consistent background removal.

prompt_templates.title: Remove background from a local image
prompt_templates.scenario: Basic usage with a local image file

prompt_templates.title: Remove background from a remote URL
prompt_templates.scenario: Processing an image from a URL with auto-download

prompt_templates.title: Specify custom output path
prompt_templates.scenario: Saving the result to a specific directory

prompt_templates.title: Check credits before processing
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the verging.ai API to check my remaining credits, then process ./team-photo.jpg if I have at least 1 credit available.
prompt_templates.scenario: Verifying credits before running a job

output_examples.output: Background removed successfully. Job ID: 456. Result URL: https://verging.ai/results/456/team-photo-transparent.png
Downloaded source image to /tmp/verging-bg-removal/portrait.png. Background removed. Result saved to ./portrait-transparent.png
Account: user@example.com. Credits remaining: 42. You can process 42 more images.

best_practices: Set your VERGING_API_KEY as an environment variable rather than passing it inline to avoid exposing it
Use the --download flag to automatically save results when working with URLs
Check your credit balance before processing batches of images
anti_patterns: Do not commit your API key to version control or share it publicly
Do not use this skill for images larger than 10MB as they will be rejected
Do not skip the credits check before submitting large batches

faq.question: What image formats are supported?
faq.answer: The skill supports JPG, JPEG, PNG, and WebP formats with a maximum file size of 10MB.

faq.question: How much does each image cost?
faq.answer: Each background removal costs 1 credit from your verging.ai account, regardless of image size.

faq.question: Where do I get an API key?
faq.answer: Visit verging.ai, log in, click your avatar in the top right, and navigate to API Keys to create one.

faq.question: Can I process images from URLs?
faq.answer: Yes, pass any publicly accessible image URL as the --image argument and the skill will download and process it.

faq.question: Where are temporary files stored?
faq.answer: Temporary files are stored in /tmp/verging-bg-removal/ and are cleaned up after processing completes.

faq.question: Is my data sent to a third party?
faq.answer: Yes, images are uploaded to verging.ai servers for AI processing. Do not use this skill with sensitive or private images unless you trust the service.
